data.title: US payments network Zelle is considering using stablecoin technology for international expansion.
data.description: US payments network Zelle is considering leveraging stablecoin technology for international expansion. "Zelle has transformed the way Americans transfer money domestically," said Cameron Fowler, CEO of Early Warning Services, which operates Zelle. "Now, we're working to provide the same fast, reliable service to consumers who use Zelle to send money internationally. Our goal is to bring Zelle's trust, speed, and convenience to consumers who need to send money internationally." Early Warning Services, which operates Zelle, said the move, details of which are still unclear, "marks a significant step forward in expanding Zelle's reach globally through the use of stablecoins." According to a report last month, Zelle has been exploring issuing its own stablecoin. According to Early Warning Services, approximately $1 trillion was transferred through Zelle last year. While other payment networks like PayPal and Wise handle a significant amount of cross-border payments, and many crypto companies are trying to compete by offering low-cost international remittance services, Zelle has a large customer base that may be interested in the ability to transfer money to other countries. (The Block)

data.title: Shiba Inu Might Be ‘Dead And Sleeping’, But Not For Long, Crypto Expert Says
data.description:
Shiba Inu (SHIB) continues to trade in a tight band, and that has kept many investors on edge. Based on reports, the token is down 45% year-to-date and 15% over the last 30 days. Related Reading: 16,000 Ancient Bitcoins Just Moved—And It’s Costing Whales Billions The memecoin’s price action has left holders wondering when — or if — a strong rebound will arrive. Volume and price swings have cooled, and market mood is leaning toward fear with the Fear & Greed Index at 30

data.title: The "100% Winning Whale" has increased its long position value to $270 million, with a floating loss of $384,000.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ai Yi, the "mysterious whale with a 100% win rate" has surpassed the size of its ETH long positions: BTC long position: 1,309.31 BTC, valued at $144 million, opened at $110,592.3; ETH long position: 33,270.78 ETH, valued at $129 million, opened at $3,897.59. The total position has reached $274 million, and has turned from profit to loss, with a floating loss of $384,000. In addition, there are 64.95 BTC in pending orders that have not been executed.

data.title: Bitget Wallet announces support for the x402 protocol
data.description: Web3 wallet Bitget Wallet announced its upcoming support for the x402 protocol to further explore and enhance the Web3 payment experience. This integration will enable users to initiate on-chain payments by scanning a QR code or clicking a link, providing a smoother and more natural on-chain payment experience. Bitget Wallet has been continuously expanding its presence in the Web3 payments space, launching a crypto payment card to promote the real-world use of crypto assets; launching a QR code payment feature for Vietnam, allowing users to directly scan and pay with crypto assets like USDT at local merchants; and integrating Solana Pay, bringing a native low-latency payment experience to the Solana ecosystem.

data.title: Ripple completes acquisition of Hidden Road, which has been renamed Ripple Prime
data.description: Ripple announced the completion of its acquisition of Hidden Road, now rebranded as Ripple Prime. Hidden Road previously provided institutions with clearing, prime brokerage, and foreign exchange (FX), digital assets, derivatives, swaps, and fixed income financing services. This acquisition will establish Ripple as the first cryptocurrency company to own and operate a global, multi-asset prime broker. Since the acquisition announcement, Ripple Prime has seen a threefold increase in business and expects further growth. Ripple Prime will enhance the utility and reach of Ripple's stablecoin, RLUSD. RLUSD is currently used as collateral for various prime brokerage products, and some derivatives clients have already chosen to hold RLUSD.
